repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry constructs this randomized-sign causal max-functional family adjacent to an exact-tie law.

repo_prior_art.cite: The closest catalogue entries stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap and stat_dose_response_minimax have different estimands and risk criteria.

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004 supplies the generic testing-to-length reduction; LuedtkeVanderLaan2016, ShiLuSong2020, WuWang2021, XuGuo2026, WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, PonomarevSemenova2024, and LiuLiuSasakiWan2025 do not state this two-sided union-honest expected-length lower bound from the explicit causal sparse mixture.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry gives a connected union-honest interval for this optimal-value pair.

published_prior_art.cite: PonomarevSemenova2024 gives uniformly valid lower confidence bands robust to non-unique policies, while LiuLiuSasakiWan2025 gives policy-value Gaussian inference under a distinct non-tie setup; neither is the requested two-sided matching interval.

repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry gives a tie-versus-near-tie expected-length phase boundary for an optimal-policy value.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem: sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound and Open-ended Question: matching-union-interval
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey omits closer public theorem-text comparators PonomarevSemenova2024 (uniformly valid optimal-welfare lower confidence bands under non-uniqueness) and LiuLiuSasakiWan2025 (nonparametric policy-value Gaussian inference); distinguish their one-sided/non-tie guarantees from this two-sided tie-union lower bound.

soundness_flags.label: lem:mean-envelope
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated premises only bound potential outcomes, but the conclusion concerns observed regressions m_{P,a}; consistency and conditional exchangeability, which identify m_{P,a}=E_F[Y(a)|X], must be added to the statement dependencies/premises.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The strict lower-bound claim is confined to the explicit two-stratum construction and 3s_1α<d; it does not claim a global adaptation diagram or attainment.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Strict slower-than-root-n expected-length penalty at P_0^∘ conditional on the displayed sparse family, fixed class parameters, and 3s_1α<d.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:matching-union-interval
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: No matching connected union-honest upper interval is asserted; it remains an explicitly labeled open-ended construction problem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The lower theorem is not represented as a sharp attained frontier unless this OEQ is resolved.

source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 1805 abstract; pp. 1808–1811, Proposition 1 and Section 2.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper studies adaptive confidence intervals for linear functionals and derives expected-length lower bounds through testing and ordered/between-class moduli.
The article characterizes pathwise differentiability of the optimal value and develops root-n confidence-interval methodology, including exceptional laws.

source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 1 abstract; pp. 5–7, Section 2.3 and Conditions (A3)–(A6)
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper develops subagging confidence intervals for the optimal value, establishes nominal coverage, and compares interval lengths under its stated conditions.
The preprint develops adaptive smoothing for the OTR value and an attained asymptotic-variance lower bound within a robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimator class.
The preprint gives softmax-smoothed optimal-policy-value inference with a constant number of nuisance fits, and its results retain nuisance consistency and convergence-rate conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: Biometrics 77 (2021), pp. 465–476, publisher abstract inspected; full text access restricted
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The accessible source states that the paper proposes model-free robust inference for optimal treatment regimes; no stronger formula-level comparison was made from inaccessible full text.
The article constructs bias-aware honest confidence intervals for scalar nonparametric-regression targets under predeclared restrictions and derives efficiency/bandwidth results.
The cited paper uses a Tsybakov margin condition together with Hölder regression and strong-density classes for plug-in classification.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 112(520) (2017), pp. 1381–1392
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article studies individualized treatment regimes for rilpivirine versus efavirenz in treatment-naive HIV patients, with baseline viral load and CD4 count as effect-modifying context.

The paper studies optimal-welfare lower confidence bands, relates non-uniformity to failure of a separation/margin condition, and constructs uniformly valid lower bands through moment-inequality tests.

The preprint develops nonparametric classification/policy-value inference using a strictly convex surrogate and proves Gaussian asymptotics for the optimal policy value under its distinct representative-policy assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a source-located comparison with PonomarevSemenova2024 and LiuLiuSasakiWan2025, explicitly separating one-sided bands and non-tie representative-policy assumptions from two-sided uniform expected-length honesty at an exact tie.
Repair lem:mean-envelope by adding consistency and conditional exchangeability to its premises and dependencies, or restate its conclusion directly for E_F[Y(a)|X].

Upgrade the field-tier lower bound to a sharp adaptation result by constructing a connected interval attaining n^{-ξ} up to logarithms over the same ordered pair, or prove a distinct binding obstruction for 3s_1α≥d.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision: repair the mean-envelope premise and broaden the literature comparison to the two omitted public policy-value inference papers; then retain the explicit sparse-mixture lower theorem as a field-tier kernel.
